            Your directive (also reproduced below for convenience) is at:
              /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/logs/iter-170/blueprint-writer-avr-orphan170-directive.md

            DIRECTIVE:
            # Blueprint-Writer Directive — Slug `avr-orphan170`

## Chapter

`blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`

## What to do (1 mechanical edit, 1 small additional cleanup)

### Edit 1 — DELETE the orphan `def:ga_grpObj` block (REQUIRED)

The Lean instance `AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj` was DELETED from `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean` in iter-169 (SECONDARY-4 hygiene). The blueprint block at `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` L1020–L1040 still pins the deleted instance via `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj}`. The iter-169 lean-vs-blueprint-checker explicitly flagged this as a must-fix orphan; the iter-169 plan-writer NOTE already attached to the block (L1025-L1032) explicitly says "the iter-170 plan-writer should DELETE this entire block".

Action: delete the entire `\begin{definition}[The additive `GrpObj` structure on `𝔾_a`] ... \end{definition}` block (currently L1020–L1040, including its `\label{def:ga_grpObj}`, `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj}` pin, `\uses{def:ga}` edge, the iter-169 `% NOTE` comment, and the trailing prose). No other block in the chapter carries an incoming `\uses{def:ga_grpObj}` edge (verified via grep), so the deletion is purely local cleanup.

Then update `blueprint/lean_decls` to remove the line `AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj` (currently line 134). Make a single-line removal; do NOT regenerate the whole file.

### Edit 2 — Refresh the iter-169 NOTE on `def:gaTranslationP1` (REQUIRED)

The block `def:gaTranslationP1` (L1164–L1223) carries an iter-169 `% NOTE` flagging that `gmScalingP1` ships `:= sorry` with three escalation options pending the iter-170 planner decision. Iter-170 has now MADE that decision: **option (c) inline chart-glue at scale, decomposed across iter-170 → iter-173 with named milestones per iter**.

Refresh the `% NOTE (iter-169)` block (L1169–L1178) to:

```latex
% NOTE (iter-170 decision): the iter-169 escalation surface CLOSED — the
% planner committed to option (c) inline chart-glue at scale (NOT option (a)
% Mathlib upstream PR, NOT option (b) accept `[CharZero]` fallback). The
% commitment is a bounded 3-4-iter decomposition of the chart-glue, with
% named per-iter milestones in `iter/iter-170/plan.md`. The Lean body of
% `gmScalingP1` still ships as a typed `sorry` THIS iter; iter-170 attacks
% Step A (chart-side ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart_i_ringMap`) + the
% `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` residual (the iso's lone sorry,
% needed for Step B). iter-171/iter-172/iter-173 carry the rest.
% See `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` for the full 6-step decomposition.
```

(Keep the surrounding `% Archon-original Lean encoding` and `% NOTE (iter-164/167)` comments — only swap the iter-169 NOTE for the iter-170 one.)

### Edit 3 — Refresh the iter-169 NOTE on `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` (REQUIRED)

The block `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` (L1225–L1240) carries an iter-169 `% NOTE` saying "gated sorry, strictly downstream". Refresh to:

```latex
% NOTE (iter-170 decision): the Lean body of `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`
% remains a gated `sorry` — its discharge is strictly downstream of
% `gmScalingP1`'s body landing (per option (c) decomposition, this means
% landing in iter-173 at the earliest). Once `gmScalingP1` is concrete via
% the chart-glue, this collapse lemma reduces to a chart-1 direct computation
% via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`.
```

## What you MUST NOT do

- Do NOT add or remove `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers anywhere. The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase manages `\leanok`; the review agent manages `\mathlibok`. Even on the deleted block, do not touch markers — just delete the whole block atomically.
- Do NOT modify any other chapter or any other block in this chapter. Targeted minimum edit only.
- Do NOT spawn a child reference-retriever or any other subagent. No external sources are needed.

## Citation discipline

The chapter ships its citation discipline already (`% SOURCE`, `% SOURCE QUOTE`, `\textit{Source: ...}`). Your edits change only the noted iter blocks and the orphan block deletion; you don't need to add new citation blocks (none of the existing blocks loses its citation under this edit).

## Verification you should do

1. After the edit, `grep -n 'def:ga_grpObj\|ga_grpObj' AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` should return zero hits.
2. After the `lean_decls` edit, `grep -n 'ga_grpObj' blueprint/lean_decls` should return zero hits.
3. The total chapter LOC drops by ~20 (the deleted block); the two NOTE refreshes are LOC-neutral.

## Output

Report at `task_results/blueprint-writer-avr-orphan170.md`. Cite the line numbers post-edit so a checker can re-verify.

## Out-of-scope flag

The lean-vs-blueprint-checker iter169 ALSO recommended (optional):
- adding per-decl `\lean{...}` pins for `projectiveLineBar_isProper` (axiom-clean) and disclosing the scaffold-sorry status of `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` / `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim` under `def:genus0_base_objects`.

These are NOT in scope this iter. Do NOT do them. They will be picked up in a future hygiene writer-pass.


            Report: /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-avr-orphan170.md
            (When invoked as a child of another subagent, your report
            lands at task_results/<parent_slug>/blueprint-writer-avr-orphan170.md
            — the Archon CLI handles the path automatically.)


## Developer feedback channel (optional)

If during this iteration you notice something that would make Archon
better — a missing capability, redundant functionality, a
prompt instruction that contradicts itself, a tool you wish existed,
new ideas for better efficiency, etc — you may leave a short note
for the developer by appending to this file with a bash heredoc:

    mkdir -p /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/.debug-feedback
    cat >> /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/.debug-feedback/debug_feedback.md <<'EOF'

    ## iter-170 · blueprint-writer (avr-orphan170)

    <your note here, one concrete observation, under ~200 words>
    EOF

Rules:
- This file is WRITE-ONLY from your perspective. Do NOT read it,
  cat it, grep it, or open it in any tool. It is for the developer.
- Only leave a note if you have something concrete to say. Empty or
  generic feedback ("everything went fine") is noise — skip it.
- One concrete observation per note. Keep it under ~200 words.
- This is optional and does not affect your task. Skip it if nothing
  comes to mind.

content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: false
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the most recent blueprint
10	    review (the blueprint-review subagent in your catalog, when
11	    present) flagged as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
12	    multi-route coverage.
13	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
14	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
15	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
16	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
17	    what the directive lists.
18	  - **NEVER instruct the writer to add ``\leanok`` or ``\mathlibok``
19	    markers** (no "after writing the block, add `\leanok`"; no
20	    "verification: confirm `\leanok` is present"). The writer's
21	    descriptor forbids it — ``\leanok`` is managed by the
22	    deterministic ``sync_leanok`` phase, ``\mathlibok`` by the review
23	    agent. A directive that asks the writer to add markers puts it in
24	    a rule conflict; the writer must obey its descriptor, so the
25	    "verification" instruction will appear to fail until sync_leanok
26	    runs. Leave marker concerns out of the writer's directive entirely.
27	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
28	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
29	    writer with TWO globs:
30	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
31	      --write-domain 'references/**'
32	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
33	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
34	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
35	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
36	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
37	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
38	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
39	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
40	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
41	  - After a significant writer round, re-run the blueprint review
42	    in the same iteration (the relevant entry in your catalog) to
43	    confirm the updated blueprint is now sufficient — do not assume
44	    the writer fixed everything.
45	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
46	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
47	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
48	    strategy-level issue.
49	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
50	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
51	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
52	---
53	
54	# Blueprint Writer
55	
56	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
57	
58	## Your Job
59	
60	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
61	
62	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
63	
64	## Directive Format
65	
66	```markdown
67	# Blueprint Writer Directive
68	
69	## Slug
70	<slug>
71	
72	## Target chapter
73	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
74	
75	## Strategy context
76	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
77	
78	## Required content
79	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
80	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
81	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
82	- ...
83	
84	## Out of scope
85	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
86	
87	## References
88	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
89	- ...
90	
91	## Expected outcome
92	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
93	```
94	
95	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
96	
97	## Chapter format
98	
99	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
100	
101	```latex
102	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
103	  \label{thm:some_label}
104	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
105	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
106	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174  (read from references/hartshorne-III-5.md)
107	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A morphism $f: X \to Y$ of schemes locally of finite
108	  % type is said to be smooth at $x \in X$ if there exist an open affine
109	  % neighborhood $V = \Spec B$ of $f(x)$ and an open affine neighborhood
110	  % $U = \Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \subset V$ such that ..."
111	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, III.5.1.}
112	  Informal statement of the theorem, in the project's notation.
113	\end{theorem}
114	
115	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Proof. We may assume $Y = \Spec B$ and
116	% $X = \Spec A$ are affine. Then $f$ corresponds to a ring homomorphism
117	% $\varphi: B \to A$, and $f$ is smooth at $x$ if and only if ..."
118	\begin{proof}
119	  \uses{thm:another_result}
120	  Step-by-step informal proof, in the project's notation. Reference blueprint labels
121	  with \uses{...} so the dependency graph stays accurate.
122	\end{proof}
123	```
124	
125	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
126	
127	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
128	
129	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
130	
131	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
132	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
133	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.
134	   - **Original notation preserved.** If the source writes $\mathcal{O}_X^*$ and the project uses $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$, the verbatim quote keeps $\mathcal{O}_X^*$. The project-notation restatement happens in the prose body that follows, NOT in the quote.
135	   - **Every word, every symbol.** No paraphrase, no "for brevity" abbreviation. The quote is the anti-hallucination signal — a writer who actually opened the source can paste; a writer reconstructing from memory cannot reproduce the source's exact words.
136	3. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` line** — first line of the prose body. Renders in the PDF so a mathematician reading the typeset blueprint sees the citation at a glance.
137	
138	**For proof blocks**: include a `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` LaTeX comment **immediately before** the `\begin{proof}` environment (NOT inside it). Same verbatim rules — original language, original notation, every word. The informal proof body inside `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` is the project's restated version in project notation, what the prover formalizes.
139	
140	When a source proof is too long for one verbatim block (multi-page construction): split the theorem into sub-lemmas in the directive's logical structure, and give each sub-statement its own `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`. One opaque mega-quote defeats verifiability. If even sub-splitting is impractical, report it in "Notes for Plan Agent" — do not silently drop the verbatim quote.
141	
142	**For Archon-original / project-bespoke results** (the directive does not name an external source for this block): the source lines are omitted — the block stands on the proof sketch alone.
143	
144	**The hard rule, explicit:**
145	
146	> **You may NEVER write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from training memory.** Every such line must be backed by a local file under `references/` that you have **opened and read in this session**, and the verbatim quote must be a character-by-character copy of text from that file.
147	
148	The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical in `% SOURCE:` is your discipline check. If, when reviewing your own draft, you find a block where you cannot point to the specific local file you read to produce the quote — you fabricated it. Delete the quote. Either dispatch a `reference-retriever` (see below) to obtain the missing source, or report the block as INCOMPLETE under "Notes for Plan Agent".
149	
150	If the directive named a source but the local reference file is missing or doesn't contain the specific statement you need:
151	
152	- **Dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** (see "Dispatching a reference-retriever" below) to fetch the missing material into `references/<slug>.md`.
153	- **Wait for the retriever to return**, then **open and read the new file**.
154	- THEN write the citation block, citing the new local file in the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
155	
156	If retrieval fails (paywall, broken link, no API key, not available online): mark the block `% SOURCE: <pointer> (verbatim text not yet retrieved)` and skip it. Report it as INCOMPLETE. Do NOT substitute a paraphrase, a recollection, or a translation as the verbatim quote — that's the iter-149 failure mode and the entire rule exists to prevent it.
157	
158	## Rules
159	
160	### What you CAN do
161	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
162	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
163	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
164	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
165	- Read `references/summary.md` and any reference that is in `references/` to ground your writing in the project's sources.
166	
167	### What you MUST do
168	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
169	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
170	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
171	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
172	- **Follow citation discipline** for every block derived from external reference material (see "Citation discipline (the hard rule)" above). Each such block needs `% SOURCE:` with a local-file parenthetical, `% SOURCE QUOTE:` with a verbatim original-language quote, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` before the proof env when applicable, and a visible `\textit{Source: ...}` prefix. Never cite from memory — only from a local `references/<file>.md` you opened and read in this session.
173	- **Document every change** in your report, including which `references/<file>.md` files you opened (under "References consulted").
174	
175	### What you MUST NOT do
176	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
177	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
178	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
179	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
180	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
181	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
182	- **Do NOT fabricate citations.** Never write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from memory. If you don't have a local `references/<file>.md` containing the source text, dispatch a retriever and wait. The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical is the discipline check — if you cannot truthfully point to the file you read, the citation block is not allowed in the chapter.
183	- **Do NOT translate or restate the verbatim quote.** `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` contain the source's original language, original notation, every word as-is. Project-notation rewrites belong in the rendered prose body, not in the verbatim comment.
184	
185	## Reading references
186	
187	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
188	
189	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
190	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
191	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
192	
193	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
194	
195	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
196	
197	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
198	
199	Conditions for dispatch:
200	
201	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
202	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
203	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
204	
205	Dispatch (Bash; treat as blocking and await the report; in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
206	
207	```
208	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
209	  --name reference-retriever \
210	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
211	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
212	  --write-domain 'references/**'
213	```
214	
215	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
216	
217	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
218	
219	## Workflow
220	
221	1. Read your directive completely.
222	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references. Track which local files you actually opened — you will need their paths for the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
223	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
224	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow. For each block that derives from an external source, identify the specific local `references/<file>.md` containing the verbatim statement (and proof, if applicable). If no local file covers the statement, list it as "retrieval needed" for the next step.
225	5. **For every "retrieval needed" item, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and **wait** for it to return. THEN open and read the newly-written `references/<slug>.md` before drafting the citing block. Do not draft citation blocks against pending or imagined sources.
226	6. Make the edits. For each citation block: copy `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` content character-by-character from the local reference file — original language, original notation, every word. Do not transcribe from a window of the file you "remember reading"; have the file open and copy.
227	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`). Spot-check that every `% SOURCE:` line has a non-empty `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical and that the named file exists.
228	8. Write your report. List under "References consulted" every local file you opened in step 2 and step 5.
229	
230	## Logging
231	
232	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
233	
234	```markdown
235	# Blueprint Writer Report
236	
237	## Slug
238	<slug>
239	
240	## Status
241	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
242	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
243	
244	## Target chapter
245	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
246	
247	## Changes Made
248	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
249	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
250	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
251	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
252	- ...
253	
254	## Cross-references introduced
255	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
256	- ...

1016	  It serves as the $W$-base point for the additive decomposition applied in the primary

1018	\end{definition}

1042	\begin{definition}
1043	[The multiplicative $\mathtt{GrpObj}$ structure on $\mathbb G_m$]
1044	  \label{def:gm_grpObj}

1047	  % Archon-original Lean encoding (per-decl pin for the multiplicative-group structure on
1048	  % \cref{def:gm}); no external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1049	  The multiplicative group-object structure $(\mathbb G_m, \cdot, 1)$ in
1050	  $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, recording the multiplication $(x, y) \mapsto xy$ and unit $1$
1051	  of \cref{def:gm} as a Lean $\mathtt{GrpObj}$ instance. This structure is consumed only as a

1056	
1057	\subsection*{Chart cover and chart-ring iso (formalisation infrastructure)}
1058	
1059	The next three declarations are iter-168 formalisation infrastructure that the chartwise
1060	construction of the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1})
1061	and its downstream consumers (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}) ultimately rest on.
1062	They realise, at the Lean level, the standard ``affine chart of $\mathbb P^1$ is $\mathbb
1063	A^1$'' picture: a two-chart affine open cover of $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ by $D_+(X_0)$ and
1064	$D_+(X_1)$; the identification of each chart ring with the one-variable polynomial ring
1065	$\bar k[u]$; and the resulting reducedness of $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ as a scheme. None
1066	of them is on the genus-$0$ critical path conceptually, but each is consumed by the Lean
1067	formalisation downstream.
1068	
1069	\begin{definition}
1070	[Two-chart affine open cover of $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$]
1071	  \label{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1072	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover}
1073	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
1074	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of the textbook fact that $\mathbb P^1$ is covered by its
1075	  % two standard affine charts $D_+(X_0)$ and $D_+(X_1)$; the construction is built on top of
1076	  % Mathlib's $\mathrm{Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan}$. No external % SOURCE QUOTE
1077	  % needed for the Lean encoding itself.
1078	  The two-chart affine open cover of $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ by the standard $D_+(X_0)$ and
1079	  $D_+(X_1)$, built by specialising Mathlib's
1080	  $\mathrm{Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan}$ to the index set
1081	  $\iota = \{0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\,2$ with family $f = (X_0, X_1)$ and weights
1082	  $m = (1, 1)$. The non-trivial input is that the irrelevant ideal of the bi-graded
1083	  $\bar k[X_0, X_1]$ is contained in $\mathrm{Ideal.span}\{X_0, X_1\}$, which holds by
1084	  writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials of strictly positive total degree
1085	  (so each monomial is divisible by $X_0$ or $X_1$). This cover packages the two affine
1086	  charts into the data that Mathlib's $\mathrm{IsReduced.of\_openCover}$ and
1087	  $\mathrm{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}$ APIs consume, and is the chartwise glue for the
1088	  iter-169 attack on the body of $\sigma_\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}$
1089	  (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}).
1090	\end{definition}
1091	
1092	\begin{definition}
1093	[Chart-ring iso: $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong \bar k[u]$]
1094	  \label{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
1095	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}
1096	  \uses{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1097	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of the textbook identification ``the affine chart of
1098	  % $\mathbb P^1$ at $X_i$ is $\mathbb A^1$ with coordinate $X_{1-i}/X_i$'', expressed at
1099	  % the homogeneous-localization level. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1100	  For each $i \in \{0, 1\}$, the chart-$i$ ring of $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ --- the
1101	  degree-zero part $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ of the localization of the standard
1102	  $\mathbb N$-graded polynomial ring $\bar k[X_0, X_1]$ at $X_i$ --- is isomorphic as a
1103	  $\bar k$-algebra to the one-variable polynomial ring $\bar k[u]$, where the affine
1104	  chart coordinate is $u = X_{1-i}/X_i$. This is the formalisation-level realisation of
1105	  the standard ``affine chart of $\mathbb P^1$ is $\mathbb A^1$'' identification expressed
1106	  at the homogeneous-localization level. The forward direction $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal
1107	  A\,X_i \to \bar k[u]$ is built via $\mathrm{Localization.awayLift}$ from the chart
1108	  evaluation $X_i \mapsto 1$, $X_{1-i} \mapsto u$; the inverse direction is built via the
1109	  universal property of $\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k$, sending $u$ to the
1110	  homogeneous-localization element $X_{1-i}/X_i \in \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$; the
1111	  two directions are packaged as a $\mathrm{RingEquiv}$ via $\mathrm{RingEquiv.ofRingHom}$
1112	  applied to the two round-trip identities.
1113	  % NOTE (iter-169): the reverse round-trip of this iso (the
1114	  % \cref{lem:proj_chart_ring_iso_aux_left} sub-lemma below) is currently a scaffold
1115	  % \texttt{sorry} on the Lean side; the iso therefore ships sorry-tainted until that
1116	  % residual closes. The forward round-trip (the identity on $\bar k[u]$) is axiom-clean.
1117	\end{definition}
1118	
1119	\begin{lemma}
1120	[Reverse round-trip of the chart-ring iso]
1121	  \label{lem:proj_chart_ring_iso_aux_left}
1122	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left}
1123	  \uses{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
1124	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; this is the residual identity needed for the chart-ring
1125	  % map of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} to upgrade from a pair of mutually inverse-looking
1126	  % ring maps to a genuine $\mathrm{RingEquiv}$. No external % SOURCE QUOTE.
1127	  The composite $\mathrm{inverse} \circ \mathrm{forward} \colon \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal
1128	  A\,X_i \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ is the identity ring map. This is the reverse
1129	  half of the round-trip witnessing that the chart-ring map of
1130	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} is a ring isomorphism; the forward half $\mathrm{forward}
1131	  \circ \mathrm{inverse} = \mathrm{id}_{\bar k[u]}$ is already proven axiom-clean.
1132	  Currently this reverse round-trip is the iso's lone residual scaffold $\mathtt{sorry}$ on
1133	  the Lean side. The recommended closure path (per the iter-168 analogist) is the
1134	  \emph{``cancel surjective''} route: prove that the inverse map $\bar k[u] \to
1135	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ is surjective (its image equals
1136	  $\mathrm{Algebra.adjoin}\,\mathcal A_0$ of the localised generator, which is $\top$ by
1137	  $\mathrm{Away.adjoin\_mk\_prod\_pow\_eq\_top}$ specialised to $d = 1$, $v = (X_0, X_1)$,
1138	  $dv = (1, 1)$) and combine surjectivity with the already-proven forward round-trip to
1139	  cancel.
1140	\end{lemma}
1141	
1142	\begin{lemma}
1143	[$\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ is reduced]
1144	  \label{lem:projlinebar_isReduced}
1145	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isReduced}
1146	  \uses{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1147	  % Archon-original Lean realisation of an elementary textbook fact (projective space over
1148	  % a field is reduced) via the explicit chart cover of \cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1149	  % and the canonical $\mathrm{val}$-injection of a homogeneous localization into the
1150	  % corresponding plain localization. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1151	  The scheme underlying $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ is reduced. By the two-chart affine cover
1152	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1153	  reduced. The chart-$i$ ring $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ embeds into the ordinary
1154	  localization $\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$ of the polynomial ring $\bar k[X_0, X_1]$ at
1155	  the powers of $X_i$ via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1156	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1157	  ($\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}$); the codomain is a localization of a
1158	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of $X_i$), hence a domain, hence
1159	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1160	  ($\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}$ on the $\mathrm{val}$-embedding), so each chart
1161	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1162	\end{lemma}
1163	
1164	\begin{definition}
1165	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ (primary), and the $\mathbb G_a$-translation companion $\sigma$]
1166	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
1167	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
1168	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
1169	  % NOTE (iter-169): ESCALATION ARMED — the Lean body of `gmScalingP1` ships as a typed
1170	  % `sorry` (5th-iter deferral, iter-165 scaffold → iter-169 PARTIAL). The iter-169 prover
1171	  % attempted three structurally distinct routes (direct `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the
1172	  % whole pullback; chart-glue Option B; functoriality `Proj.map`) — each terminated at a
1173	  % different missing Mathlib piece (loss of `\mathbb P^1`-factor via global sections;
1174	  % missing `CommRing`/`Algebra` instance on `TensorProduct (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ _) _`;
1175	  % missing relative-Proj base-change iso). The iter-170 plan will pick one of three
1176	  % escalation options (a) commit to upstream Mathlib relative-Proj PR; (b) accept
1177	  % `[CharZero]`-gated headline via `rigidity_over_kbar`; (c) inline chart-glue at scale.
1178	  % See `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_169/recommendations.md`.
1179	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
1180	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- $\mathbb G_m$ acts on
1181	  % $\mathbb P^1$ by scalings fixing $0$ and $\infty$, $\mathbb G_a$ by translations fixing $\infty$
1182	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of $\mathrm{PGL}_2$ acting on $\mathbb P^1$).
1183	  % NOTE (iter-164/167): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the
1184	  % MULTIPLICATIVE scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\lean name gmScalingP1, landed iter-165/166;
1185	  % pinned in this block's \lean{...} hook). The $\mathbb G_a$-translation $\sigma$ (\lean name
1186	  % gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive alternative and is
1187	  % intentionally not yet a Lean target.
1188	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
1189	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1190	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
1191	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1}) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
1192	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
1193	  fixing both $0$ and $\infty$) --- defined chartwise:
1194	  \begin{itemize}
1195	    \item On $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (target chart $x$): $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$,
1196	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
1197	    \item Near $\infty$ (target chart $u = 1/x$): the target coordinate $1/(\lambda x)$ rewrites as
1198	      $u/\lambda$, regular everywhere on this chart because $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$ is invertible.
1199	  \end{itemize}
1200	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so $\sigma_\times$ is defined on
1201	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group $\mathbb G_m$ on $\mathbb P^1$:
1202	  $\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x$ and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
1203	  \lambda\mu)$. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
1204	  $\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty$ for all $\lambda$; the fixed point $0$ is what makes the
1205	  $W$-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
1206	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
1207	
1208	  \medskip
1209	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the $\mathbb G_a$-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
1210	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
1211	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1212	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
1213	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
1214	  of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a$, defined chartwise: on $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a$
1215	  (chart $x$) it is the polynomial map $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$; near $\infty$ ($u = 1/x$) the
1216	  coordinate $1/(x + y)$ rewrites as $u/(1 + u y)$, regular at $u = 0$ (value $0$ there --- $\sigma$
1217	  fixes $\infty$ for every $y$), while the locus $x + y = 0$ is covered by the chart $x$ on which it
1218	  is the finite value $0$. It is a left action of $\mathbb G_a$ on $\mathbb P^1$ fixing $\infty$:
1219	  $\sigma(x, 0) = x$ and $\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')$. This companion underlies
1220	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
1221	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
1222	  genus-$0$ critical path.
1223	\end{definition}
1224	
1225	\begin{lemma}
1226	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action fixes the origin $0 \in \mathbb P^1$]
1227	  \label{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}
1228	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero}
1229	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, def:p1bar_zero, def:gm_one}
1230	  % NOTE (iter-169): GATED SORRY — the Lean body of `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` ships as
1231	  % `sorry`, strictly downstream of `gmScalingP1`'s body (`def:gaTranslationP1`) — cannot
1232	  % land before that one. Lands as a chart-1 direct computation (~15 LOC via
1233	  % `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`) once `gmScalingP1` lands. Status tracks
1234	  % the parent escalation (see NOTE on `def:gaTranslationP1`).
1235	  % Archon-original; the fact is elementary (σ_×(0, λ) = λ · 0 = 0). No external % SOURCE QUOTE
1236	  % needed for the Lean encoding.
1237	  Let $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ be the scaling action
1238	  of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Then the composite
1239	  \[ \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1},\ \mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m})\;}
1240	     \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_\times\;} \mathbb P^1 \]
1241	  equals the constant morphism $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1}$. That is,
1242	  $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ for every $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$.
1243	\end{lemma}
1244	
1245	\begin{proof}
1246	  \uses{def:gaTranslationP1, def:p1bar_zero, def:gm_one}
1247	  Chart-level. On the affine chart $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (coordinate $x$), the
1248	  morphism $\sigma_\times$ is the polynomial map $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda \cdot x$. Restricting
1249	  to the $\mathbb G_m$-section $x = 0$ gives the constant ring map

# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
avr-orphan170

## Status
COMPLETE

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex

(plus the supporting one-line edit to `blueprint/lean_decls`, which is the explicitly
named secondary cleanup in the directive.)

## Changes Made

- **Deleted block** `def:ga_grpObj` — the entire `\begin{definition}[The additive `GrpObj`
  structure on `\mathbb G_a`]...\end{definition}` block previously at L1020–L1040 was
  removed, including its `\label{def:ga_grpObj}`, `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj}` pin,
  `\uses{def:ga}` edge, the iter-169 `% NOTE` explanatory comment, and the trailing prose.
  Rationale (per directive): the Lean instance `AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj` was deleted
  from `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean` in iter-169 SECONDARY-4 hygiene, leaving
  this `\lean{...}` pin orphaned. No other block in the chapter carries an incoming
  `\uses{def:ga_grpObj}` edge (verified via grep), so the deletion is purely local cleanup.
  The `\mathbb G_a`-additive route is off-path per the iter-164 strategy pivot.

- **Removed line 134** of `blueprint/lean_decls` (`AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj`) — single-
  line removal to keep the lean-decls manifest in sync with the chapter and prevent the
  blueprint-doctor from re-flagging an orphan pin next iter. Did NOT regenerate the rest
  of the file.

- **Revised** `def:gaTranslationP1` (now L1144) — swapped the iter-169 `% NOTE
  (ESCALATION ARMED)` block for the iter-170 decision NOTE recording option (c) inline
  chart-glue as the committed route, naming Step A (chart-side ring maps
  `gmScalingP1_chart_i_ringMap`) + the `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` residual
  as the iter-170 prover-lane targets, and pointing to `iter/iter-170/plan.md` and
  `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` for the full decomposition. LOC-neutral.

- **Revised** `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` (now L1205) — swapped the iter-169 `% NOTE
  (GATED SORRY)` for the iter-170 decision NOTE: the body remains a gated `sorry`, lands
  in iter-173 at the earliest (downstream of `gmScalingP1`'s body landing per option (c)),
  reduces to a chart-1 direct computation via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`
  once `gmScalingP1` is concrete. LOC-neutral.

## Cross-references introduced

None added. One incoming edge removed by deletion: the `\uses{def:ga}` outbound from the
deleted `def:ga_grpObj` block. The deletion is sink-side (no other block referenced
`def:ga_grpObj`), so no other `\uses{...}` had to be edited.

## Verification (post-edit line numbers)

1. `grep -n 'def:ga_grpObj\|ga_grpObj' blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`
   → zero hits (was 6 hits before).
2. `grep -n 'ga_grpObj' blueprint/lean_decls` → zero hits (was 1 hit on L134 before).
3. Web artifacts under `blueprint/web/` still mention `def:ga_grpObj` (HTML / dep_graph)
   — these are generated outputs and will refresh when `leanblueprint` rebuilds them; the
   sources of truth (`*.tex` + `lean_decls`) are clean. Not in scope to regenerate manually.
4. Post-edit anchor lines on the touched blocks:
   - `def:gm_grpObj` now at L1022 (shifted up from L1044 by the 22-line deletion above).
   - `def:gaTranslationP1` now at L1144 (shifted up from L1166 by the deletion).
   - `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` now at L1205 (shifted up from L1227 by the deletion).
   - The two refreshed `% NOTE (iter-170 decision)` blocks are at L1145–L1154 (inside
     `def:gaTranslationP1`) and L1208–L1213 (inside `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero`).
5. Chapter LOC delta: −22 lines (the deleted block, including its blank trailing line);
   the two NOTE swaps are LOC-neutral.

## References consulted

(none — this is a mechanical orphan-deletion + NOTE-refresh pass; no external source
material is involved, no new `% SOURCE` blocks are added, and no existing `% SOURCE`
block lost its citation under this edit. The directive explicitly states no citation
work is needed.)

## Macros needed (if any)

None.

## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)

None — the directive explicitly forbade spawning child subagents this round.

## Notes for Plan Agent

- The web artifacts under `blueprint/web/chap-AbelianVarietyRigidity.html` and
  `blueprint/web/dep_graph_document.html` still carry stale `def:ga_grpObj` references
  (lines 1665/1677/1712 in the chapter HTML; lines 272/278/287/290 + the long line 6117
  in `dep_graph_document.html`). These are generated artifacts that refresh on next
  `leanblueprint build`. I did NOT touch them — `blueprint/web/**` is generated output,
  not source, and a manual edit would just be overwritten. Flagging in case the dashboard
  surface care about a forced rebuild.
- The directive's out-of-scope flag (per-decl `\lean{...}` pins for
  `projectiveLineBar_isProper` and scaffold-sorry disclosure for
  `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` / `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`) is acknowledged and
  explicitly deferred to a future hygiene writer-pass per the directive's instruction.
  Not addressed this round.

## Strategy-modifying findings

(none — the edits are purely hygiene + NOTE refresh; no strategy-level issue surfaced.)
